response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and backend engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js experience and solid software craftsmanship practices, but critically misses the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ programming, yet his resume shows only roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and a single bachelor thesis project — a gap that is disqualifying for a role where C++ is the foundational technical requirement. His cloud infrastructure exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker) is real but peripheral, not constituting 5 years of large-scale infrastructure ownership or platform engineering. While he holds Staff-level titles and demonstrates mentorship instincts through workshops and conference talks, there is no evidence of formal technical leadership over cloud or platform engineering teams.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture demonstrated consistently across multiple roles: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and pairing practices
Distributed and high-throughput systems exposure at Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O architectures
Community mentorship and knowledge-sharing through recurring workshops, FOSS4G conference talks, and internal code katas aligns with the mentoring dimension of the role
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately 6 months at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis project — against a hard minimum requirement of 8 years
No demonstrable ownership of large-scale cloud compute or infrastructure platforms; cloud exposure is limited to AWS CDK for a pricing microservice and Kubernetes for a side project
No evidence of formal technical leadership over engineering teams — Staff titles appear to reflect individual contributor seniority rather than team or organizational leadership

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and high-throughput backend experience
